unsavedChanges.title: Unsaved changes
unsavedChanges.description: You have unsaved changes that will be lost if you close this dialog. Are you sure you want to discard them?

errors.backendUnavailable: Unable to reach the server. Please check that the backend is running and try again.

login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Sign in to your KinBot workspace
login.forgotPassword: Forgot your password? Contact your workspace admin.

identity.title: Your identity
identity.subtitle: Tell us about yourself
identity.avatarHint: Click to upload or drag & drop
identity.pseudonymHint: How your Kins will address you

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.subtitle: Customize your experience
preferences.themeHint: You can change this anytime in settings

providers.title: AI Providers
providers.subtitle: Configure at least one AI provider to power your Kins
providers.name: Display name
providers.requiredHint: You need at least LLM and Embedding capabilities to continue
providers.cannotFinish: Configure providers to cover LLM and Embedding capabilities
providers.nameTip: A friendly label to identify this provider in your dashboard. If left empty, the provider type name is used.
providers.baseUrlTip: Override the default API endpoint. Useful for self-hosted instances, proxies, or compatible third-party APIs.
providers.guidanceIntro: Not sure where to start? Here are the most popular options:
providers.guidanceFooter: You can add more providers later in Settings. Pick one to get started!
providers.quickFinishHint: Memory and search can be configured later in Settings

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: Choose how your Kins remember things from past conversations
memory.extractionModelHint: Reads conversations and picks out what's worth remembering. A fast, affordable model works great here. Leave empty to let each Kin use its own model.
memory.extractionModelTip: This model scans your conversations after they happen and decides which facts, preferences, and decisions should be saved as long-term memories. It doesn't need to be a powerful model, a small and fast one does the job well.
memory.embeddingModelHint: Turns memories into searchable data so your Kins can find relevant info later. A small, cheap model is perfect for this.
memory.embeddingModelTip: This model converts text into numbers (vectors) that allow your Kins to search their memories by meaning, not just keywords. For example, a memory about "loving pasta" would match a question about "favorite foods". A smaller model works fine here.

searchProviders.title: Search Providers
searchProviders.subtitle: Enable web search for your Kins (optional)

hub.defaultDescription: A central coordinator that receives all messages and routes them to specialized Kins based on their expertise. Friendly, efficient, and good at understanding intent.
hub.channelHint: Messages from this channel will go to the Hub, which can route them to specialist Kins.
hub.deleteWarning: This Kin is your Hub. Deleting it will remove your central coordinator.

wizard.title: Create your Hub
wizard.subtitle: The Hub is the central coordinator of your Kin team. It receives your messages, understands what you need, and delegates to the right specialist.
wizard.diagramCaption: You talk to the Hub. It routes to the right Kin automatically.
wizard.grantAllToolsDescription: The Hub will have access to every tool, including administrative ones (Kin management, system, database). Uncheck to let the AI decide which tools to enable.

kins.title: Kins
kins.emptyDescription: Create your first AI assistant to get started

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.emptyDescription: Tasks will appear here when Kins are working

crons.title: Scheduled Jobs
crons.emptyDescription: Schedule recurring tasks for your Kins

miniApps.title: Mini-Apps
miniApps.emptyDescription: Ask a Kin to create one
miniApps.noKinSelectedDescription: Select a Kin to see its mini-apps

edit.title: Edit Kin
edit.slugHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Used in URLs and inter-Kin communication.

create.title: Create a new Kin
create.name: Name
create.providerHint: Multiple providers can serve this model. Choose which one this Kin should use, or leave on Auto to use the first available.
create.characterHint: Define personality, tone, and communication style. This shapes how the Kin speaks and behaves in conversations.
create.expertiseHint: Describe what the Kin knows and what it should focus on. This guides its responses and areas of competence.
create.memoryEmptyDescription: Once your Kin is created, it will automatically build memories from conversations. You can also add memories manually from the edit dialog.
create.nameHint: A short, memorable name for this Kin.
create.roleHint: A brief description of what this Kin does (shown in the sidebar).
create.nameTip: A short, recognizable name. This is how the Kin appears in the sidebar and in conversations.
create.roleTip: A one-line summary of what this Kin does. Shown under the name in the sidebar.
create.modelTip: The AI model powering this Kin. Different models have different strengths - larger models are smarter but slower and more expensive.
create.characterTip: Defines the Kin's personality: how it talks, its tone, quirks, and communication style. Think of it as the Kin's "soul".
create.expertiseTip: Describes what the Kin knows and should focus on. This guides its answers, priorities, and areas of competence.

settings.title: Kin settings
settings.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? This will permanently delete this Kin and all its data.

tools.hubNotice: This Kin is the Hub — all opt-in tools are automatically enabled at runtime, regardless of the toggles below.
tools.saveMcpHint: Save the Kin first to configure MCP tools.
tools.connectionFailed: Connection failed - unable to discover tools from this server.
tools.searchProviderDescription: Override the default search provider for this Kin.

defaults.character:
You are a friendly, thoughtful, and versatile assistant. You communicate clearly and adapt your tone to the context - casual when appropriate, precise when needed.

You are patient, curious, and genuinely enjoy helping. When you don't know something, you say so honestly. You ask clarifying questions when the request is ambiguous.

You keep your responses concise unless the user explicitly asks for detail.
defaults.expertise:
You have broad general knowledge and can help with a wide range of topics:
- Writing, editing, and summarizing text
- Research, analysis, and problem-solving
- Brainstorming and creative tasks
- Explaining complex concepts simply
- Planning and organizing

You adapt your level of detail and technicality based on what the user needs.

avatar.title: Choose avatar
avatar.autoDescription: The Kin generates its own portrait based on its identity. Its name, role, and personality all influence the result - make sure they're filled in first.
avatar.noImageProvider: No image provider configured. Add one in Settings.
avatar.createFirst: Save the Kin first to enable generation.
avatar.uploadHint: Click to select or drag & drop an image (any size)

wizard.title: Describe your Kin
wizard.subtitle: Describe the assistant you need and we'll configure it for you.
wizard.placeholder: e.g. An expert in corporate law who can help draft contracts and advise on compliance...
wizard.refinePlaceholder: e.g. Make it more formal, add tax law expertise...
wizard.generateError: Failed to generate configuration. Please try again or create manually.

kin.modelUnavailableHint: This Kin's model is no longer available. Update the model in Kin settings or add the provider back.
kin.modelUnavailableInput: Cannot send messages - this Kin's model is unavailable
kin.deleteConfirm: This action cannot be undone. All conversations and data associated with this Kin will be permanently deleted.

create.title: Create scheduled job
create.name: Name
create.taskDescriptionPlaceholder: What should the Kin do at each execution...
create.targetKinHint: If set, this Kin executes the task instead of the owner.
create.modelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the Kin's default
create.nameTip: A descriptive name to identify this job. Shown in the sidebar and notifications.
create.kinTip: The Kin that owns and runs this job. It will execute the task instructions on each trigger.
create.scheduleTip: Standard cron expression (5 fields). Use the presets below or write your own. Times are in the server's timezone.
create.taskDescriptionTip: Detailed instructions for the Kin. Be specific about what it should do, check, or produce at each execution.
create.targetKinTip: Optionally delegate execution to a different Kin. Useful when the owner Kin dispatches work to a specialist.
create.modelTip: Override the Kin's default model for this job. Useful for running cheaper models on routine tasks.
create.scheduleInvalid: Invalid cron expression. Use format: minute hour day month weekday
create.oneTimeHelp: Pick a date and time. The job will fire once and then deactivate.
create.datetimePast: Datetime must be in the future

edit.title: Edit scheduled job
edit.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this scheduled job and stop all future executions.

chat.selectKin: Select a Kin to start chatting
chat.contextUsage: Context: ~{{tokens}} / {{max}} tokens ({{percent}}%), includes system prompt, history & tools
chat.contextNoData: Context usage will appear after the first message is processed

compacting.title: Compacting conversation
compacting.nothingToCompact: Nothing to compact - not enough messages yet

welcome.title: Welcome to KinBot
welcome.description: Kins are AI personalities you create and customize. Each Kin has its own character, expertise, tools, and memory. Create your first one to get started!
welcome.checklistIntro: Get started in a few simple steps. KinBot will guide you through setting up your AI team.
welcome.step1Desc: Add an AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) with your API key. This powers your Kins’ intelligence.
welcome.step2Desc: The Hub is your central coordinator. It receives messages and routes them to the right specialist Kin.
welcome.step3Desc: Create a Kin with specific expertise (finance, coding, writing...) that the Hub can delegate to.
welcome.step4Desc: Optionally connect Telegram, Discord, Slack, or other platforms so your Kins can chat outside the web UI.
welcome.allDone: You’re all set! Select a Kin from the sidebar to start chatting.

emptyState.defaultRole: Your AI assistant, ready to help.
suggestions: What can you help me with?
emptyState.hint: Type a message below or pick a suggestion to get started

clear.title: Clear conversation
clear.description: This will permanently delete all messages in this conversation. Memories and files will be preserved. This action cannot be undone.

stats.activityAriaLabel: Message activity sparkline showing conversation frequency over time

quickChat.title: Quick Session
quickChat.empty: Start a quick, private conversation with this Kin. Messages won't appear in the main session.

closing.title: Close quick session?
closing.description: This conversation will not be saved in the main history. You can optionally save a summary as a memory.
closing.summaryPlaceholder: Describe what was discussed or decided...

history.title: Session History
history.empty: No past sessions yet. Closed quick sessions will appear here.

general.title: General
general.description: Platform-wide settings that apply to all Kins.
general.globalPromptPlaceholder: Enter instructions that all Kins should follow...
general.globalPromptHint: This text is injected into every Kin's system prompt.
general.globalPromptTip: Instructions injected into every Kin's system prompt. Use this for rules or guidelines all your Kins should follow.
general.hubKinTip: The Hub Kin is the central coordinator that routes messages and manages other Kins. It gets access to all tools automatically.
general.hubKinHint: The Hub Kin receives messages by default and can delegate to specialist Kins.

help.content: General settings control platform-wide behavior that applies to all your Kins. The global prompt lets you define instructions every Kin follows.
help.bullet1: The global prompt is injected into every Kin's system prompt. Use it for universal rules or guidelines

providers.title: AI Providers
providers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?
providers.emptyDescription: Add an AI provider like OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini to power your Kins.

help.content: AI providers are the services that power your Kins. Each provider gives access to language models, embedding models, or image generation capabilities.
help.bullet1: Add multiple providers to access different models (GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.)
help.bullet2: Each Kin can use a different model depending on your needs
help.bullet3: OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, Together, etc.) work with the same configuration pattern
help.bullet4: Test the connection after adding to verify the API key is valid

searchProviders.title: Search
searchProviders.emptyDescription: Connect a search provider so your Kins can look things up on the web.
searchProviders.defaultProviderDescription: Used by all Kins unless they override it individually.

help.content: Search providers give your Kins the ability to look things up on the web during conversations. When a Kin needs current information, it uses the configured search provider to find answers.
help.bullet1: Add providers like Brave Search, Tavily, Serper, or Perplexity for web search capabilities
help.bullet2: Set a default provider or let KinBot automatically pick the first valid one
help.bullet3: Individual Kins can override the default search provider in their settings
help.bullet4: Most search providers require an API key from the provider's website

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.description: Manage Model Context Protocol servers that provide external tools to your Kins.
mcp.name: Name
mcp.deleteConfirm: This will permanently remove this MCP server and disconnect it from all Kins.
mcp.emptyDescription: MCP servers extend your Kins with external tools and data sources.
mcp.nameTip: A friendly name to identify this server in the list.
mcp.commandTip: The executable to run. Usually npx for npm packages, or an absolute path to a binary.
mcp.argsTip: Command-line arguments passed to the server. Put each argument on its own line.
mcp.envTip: Environment variables passed to the server process. Use these for API keys and secrets.
mcp.envPreserveHint: Leave values empty to keep existing secrets

help.content: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers are external programs that give your Kins additional capabilities beyond their built-in tools. They run as separate processes and communicate via a standard protocol.
help.bullet1: Each MCP server provides one or more tools (e.g. file search, database queries, API integrations)
help.bullet2: Servers are started automatically when a Kin needs them and stopped when idle
help.bullet3: Environment variables let you pass API keys and secrets securely to the server process
help.bullet4: Kins can also discover and add MCP servers on their own (shown as "Pending approval")

vault.title: Vault
vault.description: Manage encrypted entries accessible by all Kins.
vault.descriptionPlaceholder: What is this entry used for?
vault.deleteConfirm: This will permanently remove this entry and its attachments. Kins using it will no longer have access.
vault.emptyDescription: Store API keys, credentials, cards, notes and files securely.
vault.manageTypesDescription: Create custom entry types with specific field schemas.
vault.typeFormIncomplete: Name, slug, and at least one field with name and label are required
vault.deleteAttachmentConfirm: Permanently delete this attachment? This cannot be undone.
vault.deleteTypeConfirm: Delete this custom type? Existing entries of this type will be kept but no new ones can be created.
vault.keyTip: A unique identifier for this entry (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN). Kins reference entries by this key.
vault.entryTypeTip: The type determines which fields are available. Choose "Text" for simple values or a structured type for multi-field entries.
vault.descriptionTip: A short note about what this entry is used for. Helps Kins understand when to use it.

help.content: The Vault is a secure key-value store where you can save credentials, API keys, and other secrets. Kins can access vault entries through their tools without the actual secret values appearing in conversations.
help.bullet1: Secrets are encrypted at rest and only decrypted when a Kin needs them
help.bullet2: You can organize secrets by type (API key, password, token, etc.) and create custom types
help.bullet3: Attach files to vault entries for certificates, config files, or other sensitive documents
help.bullet4: Mark entries as favorites for quick access

files.title: Files
files.description: Manage files shared by Kins via shareable URLs.
files.addHint: Upload a file to the shared storage
files.editHint: Update file metadata and access settings
files.name: Display name
files.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this file and invalidate its share URL.
files.emptyDescription: Upload files that your Kins can reference during conversations.

help.content: File Storage lets your Kins save, read, and share files during conversations. Files uploaded by users or generated by Kins are stored here and can be referenced across sessions.
help.bullet1: Each Kin has its own file namespace to avoid conflicts
help.bullet2: Supports images, documents, code files, and any other file type
help.bullet3: Files can be shared in channel messages and webhook payloads

files.kinTip: The Kin that owns this file. Only this Kin can access it unless the file is public.
files.publicTip: Public files get a shareable URL. Private files are only accessible by the owning Kin.
files.passwordTip: Protect the file with a password. Anyone accessing the file will need to enter it.
files.expiresInTip: Auto-delete the file after this many hours. Leave empty to keep it indefinitely.
files.readAndBurnTip: The file is automatically deleted after being downloaded once. Useful for sensitive data.

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: View and manage all Kin memories.
memories.modelConfigDescription: Models used for memory extraction and semantic search.
memories.extractionModelHint: Reads your conversations and picks out what's worth remembering. A fast, affordable model works great here.
memories.extractionModelTip: This model scans conversations and decides which facts, preferences, and decisions should be saved as long-term memories. A small, fast model does the job well. If unset, each Kin uses its own chat model.
memories.embeddingModelHint: Turns memories into searchable data so your Kins can find relevant info by meaning, not just keywords.
memories.embeddingModelTip: Converts text into numbers (vectors) so your Kins can search memories by meaning. For example, a memory about "loving pasta" matches a question about "favorite foods". Changing this model requires re-processing all existing memories.
memories.embeddingModelWarning: Warning: changing the embedding model requires regenerating all existing memories.
memories.reembedDescription: Regenerate all memory embeddings using the current embedding model. Use this after changing the embedding model.
memories.reembedConfirm: This will re-embed all memories. It may take a while and will use your embedding API quota. Continue?
memories.reembedFailed: Re-embedding finished with errors: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed out of {{total}}
memories.subject: Subject
memories.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this memory. The Kin will no longer recall it.
memories.saveKinFirst: Save the Kin first to manage its memories.
memories.kinTip: Which Kin this memory belongs to. Each Kin has its own memory space.
memories.contentTip: The information to remember. Be specific, the Kin will recall this verbatim during conversations.
memories.categoryTip: Categorize the memory for better organization and retrieval.
memories.subjectTip: Optional tag to group related memories (e.g. a person's name, a project, a topic).
memories.emptyDescription: Memories help your Kins remember important information across conversations. Add one manually or let your Kins create them automatically.

help.content: Memories let your Kins remember important things from past conversations. Set up the models that handle remembering and searching, and manage all memories here.
help.bullet1: The extraction model reads past conversations and saves the important bits as long-term memories
help.bullet2: The embedding model makes memories searchable by meaning, so your Kin finds relevant info even if the exact words differ
help.bullet3: You don't need expensive models here. Small, fast models handle extraction and embedding just fine
help.bullet4: If no extraction model is set, each Kin uses its own chat model for memory extraction

contacts.title: Contacts
contacts.description: Manage the shared contact registry accessible by all Kins.
contacts.name: Name
contacts.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this contact and all associated identifiers and notes.
contacts.deleteWarnPlatforms: This will also revoke access from {{count}} connected platform(s) ({{platforms}}).
contacts.linkToKinTip: Link this contact to an existing Kin. Helps the prompt builder resolve the Kin's identity.
contacts.emptyDescription: Add contacts so your Kins can recognize and personalize interactions.
contacts.revokePlatformIdConfirm: Revoke this platform access? The contact will no longer be able to interact via this platform.
contacts.typeTip: "Human" for real people, "Kin" for other AI agents. This helps Kins understand who they are talking to.
contacts.linkToUserTip: Link this contact to an existing KinBot user account. Lets Kins recognize them across channels.
contacts.identifiersTip: Platform-specific identifiers (email, Telegram ID, Discord username, etc.) so Kins can recognize this contact on different channels.

help.content: The contact registry lets your Kins recognize the people they interact with across different platforms and personalize their responses.
help.bullet1: Link platform identifiers (Telegram, Discord, etc.) so the Kin automatically recognizes users
help.bullet2: Kins can add private notes on each contact to remember context
help.bullet3: Link a contact to a KinBot user account to unify identity across all channels
help.bullet4: Contacts of type "Kin" represent other AI agents in multi-agent conversations

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Manage incoming webhooks that allow external services to notify your Kins.
webhooks.name: Name
webhooks.tokenWarning: Save this token now - it will not be shown again.
webhooks.regenerateConfirm: This will invalidate the current token. External services using it will stop working until updated.
webhooks.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this webhook. External services will receive 404 errors.
webhooks.emptyDescription: Create webhooks to trigger actions or send data to external services.
webhooks.nameTip: A descriptive name for this webhook. Helps you identify it when you have multiple webhooks.
webhooks.descriptionTip: Optional note explaining what this webhook is used for. Visible only to you in the dashboard.

help.content: Webhooks let external services send events directly to your Kins via HTTP POST requests. Each webhook gets a unique URL and token that you configure in the external service.
help.bullet1: Use webhooks to connect CI/CD pipelines, monitoring alerts, form submissions, or any HTTP-capable service
help.bullet2: Each webhook is linked to a specific Kin that will process the incoming events
help.bullet3: The webhook token must be included as a Bearer token in the Authorization header
help.bullet4: The request body (JSON or text) is forwarded as a message to the target Kin

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage platform users and send invitations.
users.deleteConfirm: This will permanently remove this user's account. They will lose access to the platform.
users.cannotDeleteSelf: You cannot remove your own account

invitations.title: Invitations
invitations.label: Label
invitations.revokeConfirm: This will invalidate the invitation link. Anyone who already has it will not be able to use it.

users.emptyDescription: Invite others to collaborate and use your Kins.

help.content: Manage users who have access to your KinBot instance. Invite friends or colleagues so they can chat with your Kins.
help.bullet1: Create invitation links with a configurable expiration time
help.bullet2: Each user has their own account and conversations
help.bullet3: Invitations can be revoked at any time before they are used

channels.title: Channels
channels.description: Connect your Kins to external messaging platforms. Messages will be routed to the selected Kin, and responses sent back automatically.
channels.name: Name
channels.botTokenHint: The token will be encrypted and stored in the vault.
channels.allowedChatIdsHint: Leave empty to accept messages from all chats.
channels.autoCreateContactsHint: Automatically create a contact for unknown senders.
channels.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete the channel and its user mappings. External platform users will no longer be able to reach this Kin.

approve.title: Approve user
approve.description: {{name}} from {{platform}} requests access to this channel.

channels.emptyDescription: Set up a channel like Telegram or Discord so your Kins can chat outside the web UI.

setupGuide.title: How to get your bot token

telegram.title: Telegram Setup
steps: Send /newbot and follow the prompts to name your bot
BotFather will give you a token like 123456:ABC-DEF1234ghIkl-zyx57W2v1u123ew11
Copy that token and paste it above
telegram.tip: You can customize your bot later with /setdescription and /setuserpic in BotFather.

discord.title: Discord Setup
steps: Go to discord.com/developers/applications and click "New Application"
Go to the Bot tab and click "Reset Token" to get your token
Enable the Message Content intent under Privileged Gateway Intents
Go to OAuth2 > URL Generator, select "bot" scope with Send Messages permission
Use the generated URL to invite the bot to your server
discord.tip: Make sure the Message Content intent is enabled, or your bot won't receive message text.

channels.nameTip: A friendly name for this channel connection. Helps distinguish multiple channels of the same platform.
channels.kinTip: The Kin that will respond to messages on this channel. Each channel is handled by exactly one Kin.

help.content: Channels connect your Kins to external messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, and more. Each channel routes messages from one platform to a specific Kin.
help.bullet1: Each channel is linked to one Kin that handles all incoming messages
help.bullet2: Test your connection after setup to verify the bot token is valid
help.bullet3: Manage user approvals to control who can chat with your Kin on each platform
help.bullet4: Use allowed chat IDs to restrict which groups or chats the Kin responds to

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Choose which notifications you want to receive.
notifications.noContactsForPlatform: No contacts have a {{platform}} ID. Add a platform ID to a contact first.
notifications.label: Label
notifications.soundDescription: Play a chime when a new notification arrives.
notifications.noAvailableChannels: No active channels available. Create and activate a channel first.
notifications.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete this notification channel. You will no longer receive notifications through it.
notifications.sourceChannelTip: The messaging channel (bot) used to send notifications. Must be active and configured.
notifications.recipientTip: The contact who will receive notifications via DM. They must have a platform ID matching the selected channel.
notifications.labelTip: A friendly name to identify this delivery channel in the list.
notifications.noChannelsDescription: Add a delivery channel to receive notifications on Telegram, Discord, email, or other platforms.

help.content: Notifications keep you informed about important events in KinBot. You can toggle individual notification types on or off, and set up external delivery channels to receive them on platforms like Telegram, Discord, or email.
help.bullet1: Toggle notification types to control what you see in the notification bell
help.bullet2: External delivery channels forward notifications to your preferred platforms
help.bullet3: Each delivery channel can filter by notification type for fine-grained control
help.bullet4: Use the test button on delivery channels to verify they work correctly

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Manage plugins that extend KinBot with new tools, hooks, and integrations.

empty.title: No plugins installed
empty.description: Drop a plugin folder into the plugins/ directory and click Reload.

plugins.installDescription: Install a plugin from a Git repository or npm package.
plugins.uninstallDescription: Are you sure you want to uninstall "{{name}}"? This will remove all plugin files and data.

marketplace.title: Browse Plugins
marketplace.description: Discover and install community plugins from the registry.
marketplace.incompatibleDetail: This plugin requires KinBot {range}, but you are running {version}. It may not work correctly.
marketplace.author: Author
marketplace.noReadme: No README available for this plugin.

empty.title: No plugins found
empty.description: No plugins match your search criteria. Try adjusting your filters.

marketplace.storeDescription: Curated plugins included in the KinBot repository. These are reviewed and maintained by the KinBot team.

storeEmpty.title: No store plugins
storeEmpty.description: No curated plugins are available in the store yet.

descriptions.prompt-pending: A Kin is waiting for your response to continue
descriptions.channel-user-pending: A new user on a channel needs your approval
descriptions.cron-pending-approval: A scheduled job created by a Kin needs your approval
descriptions.mcp-pending-approval: An MCP server added by a Kin needs your approval
descriptions.kin-error: A Kin encountered an error that may need investigation
descriptions.kin-alert: A Kin sent you a notification
descriptions.mention: Someone mentioned you in a conversation

password.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

viewer.title: Tool Calls
viewer.empty: No tool calls yet. Tools will appear here when the Kin uses them.

renderers.fileTruncated: File truncated. Use offset/limit to read more.

invite.title: Join KinBot
invite.subtitle: You've been invited to join this workspace
invite.invalidToken: This invitation link is invalid or has expired.
invite.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

commandPalette.title: Command Palette
commandPalette.description: Search for commands, kins, and settings...

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Press ? anywhere to show this dialog

errorBoundary.message: Something went wrong. Please reload the page.

miniApps.deleteConfirm: "{{name}}" and all its files will be permanently deleted.

errors.closeFailed: Failed to close session. Please try again.
